backup_strategies.name: Incremental backups
backup_strategies.description: Capture only changed data blocks since the last backup to reduce backup windows and storage consumption while maintaining frequent restore points.

backup_strategies.name: Snapshot-only backups
backup_strategies.description: Use lightweight storage snapshots on a scheduled basis for environments that prioritize minimal storage usage over granular restore options.

backup_strategies.name: Full daily backups
backup_strategies.description: Create a complete backup of the entire database every day to simplify restores at the cost of higher storage and longer backup windows.

backup_strategies.name: Point-in-time recovery with incrementals
backup_strategies.description: Combine base full backups with incremental log capture to support point-in-time restores for mission-critical production workloads.

error_codes.title: SSL handshake failed
error_codes.description: The server terminated the connection during the SSL handshake phase, typically due to certificate, protocol, or cipher mismatches.

error_codes.title: Connection refused
error_codes.description: The client could not establish a TCP connection to the database service endpoint.

error_codes.title: Replication lag exceeded threshold
error_codes.description: A replica has fallen behind the primary beyond the configured replication lag threshold.

error_codes.title: Backup job failed
error_codes.description: A scheduled or on-demand backup task terminated with an error before completion.

error_codes.title: Query execution timeout
error_codes.description: The server cancelled a query because it exceeded the configured statement timeout.

error_codes.title: Storage volume full
error_codes.description: The database could not allocate additional space on the underlying storage volume.

code_snippets.title: Basic SSL connection
Python SSL connection example
code_snippets.content:
import ssl
from acme_db import Client

context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile="/etc/ssl/certs/acme-db-ca.pem")

client = Client(
    host="db.my-company.example",
    port=5432,
    user="app_user",
    password=os.environ["DB_PASSWORD"],
    ssl=context,
)

client.connect()
print("Connected over SSL")

code_snippets.title: User activity table with json and timestamp columns
code_snippets.content:
CREATE TABLE user_activity (
  id              BIGINT GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id         BIGINT        NOT NULL,
  session_id      UUID          NOT NULL,
  event_type      VARCHAR(64)   NOT NULL,
  event_properties JSONB        NOT NULL,
  request_context  JSONB        NULL,
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ   NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  processed_at    TIMESTAMPTZ   NULL
);

-- event_properties stores the raw event payload.
-- created_at and processed_at are used for ingestion and processing SLAs.

code_snippets.title: Basic orders table
Command-line usage for max_clients
code_snippets.content:
# Start the server with max_clients set to 8000
acmedb-server \
  --data-dir=/var/lib/acmedb \
  --config=/etc/acmedb/conf.yml \
  --max_clients=8000

code_snippets.title: Optimized JOIN with index hint (Python, v4.8)
code_snippets.content:
from acme_db import Client

client = Client.from_dsn("acmedb://app_user@primary/db")

sql = """
SELECT /*+ INDEX(u idx_users_org_id) INDEX(o idx_orders_org_created_at) */
       u.id AS user_id,
       u.email,
       o.id AS order_id,
       o.total_cents
FROM   users u
JOIN   orders o
  ON   o.user_id = u.id
WHERE  u.org_id = %s
  AND  o.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
ORDER BY o.created_at DESC
LIMIT  100;
"""

rows = client.query(sql, params=[1234])
for row in rows:
    print(row["user_id"], row["order_id"], row["total_cents"])

code_snippets.title: Baseline JOIN without index hints
code_snippets.content:
sql = """
SELECT u.id AS user_id, u.email, o.id AS order_id, o.total_cents
FROM   users u
JOIN   orders o
  ON   o.user_id = u.id
WHERE  u.org_id = %s
ORDER BY o.created_at DESC
LIMIT 100;
"""

code_snippets.title: Create cluster (1 primary, 2 replicas)
code_snippets.content:
# Create a replication cluster with 1 primary and 2 replicas
acmedbctl cluster create prod-repl \
  --preset "1 primary, 2 replicas" \
  --region us-east-1 \
  --tags env=prod,tier=standard

code_snippets.title: Create cluster (1 primary, 1 replica)
code_snippets.content:
# Create a replication cluster with 1 primary and 1 replica
acmedbctl cluster create staging-repl \
  --preset "1 primary, 1 replica" \
  --region us-west-2 \
  --tags env=staging

code_snippets.title: Pre-migration checklist (MySQL  full)
code_snippets.content:
1. Inventory all MySQL databases, schemas, and users that will be migrated.
2. Verify that all tables use supported storage engines and character sets.
3. Measure current peak QPS, connection counts, and database size for capacity planning.
4. Identify application features that rely on MySQL-specific behavior (e.g., implicit type casts).
5. Enable and validate backups on the source MySQL instances and document the rollback plan.
6. Set up a non-production target cluster and run a trial migration with representative data.
7. Communicate the migration window, expected impact, and rollback plan to all stakeholders.

code_snippets.title: How to resolve DB-1042 in Production
code_snippets.content:
1. Confirm that the client is configured to use TLS 1.2 or later and that insecure protocols are disabled.
2. Verify that the client trusts the server CA by checking the configured ca/ca_path against the certificate chain deployed on the database nodes.
3. Ensure that the certificate common name (CN) or subject alternative name (SAN) matches the hostname used in the client connection string.
4. Check the database server logs for DB-1042 entries to identify whether the failure is due to protocol mismatch, unsupported cipher suites, or expired certificates.
5. If certificates are expired or about to expire, rotate them following the certificate rotation guide and restart affected nodes during a maintenance window.
6. After making changes, test connectivity from a bastion host in the same network segment before re-enabling traffic from application nodes.

code_snippets.title: How to resolve DB-1305 (Backup job failed)
code_snippets.content:
- Check the backup job logs for storage or network errors.
- Verify that the target backup bucket is reachable and that credentials are valid.
- Confirm that the cluster has sufficient free space for staging temporary backup files.
- Re-run the backup job manually after addressing the underlying issue.

code_snippets.title: Basic connection (no SSL)
Minimal user activity table
Create cluster (3-node multi-primary)
code_snippets.content:
# Create a 3-node multi-primary cluster
acmedbctl cluster create global-mp \
  --preset "3-node multi-primary" \
  --region eu-central-1 \
  --tags env=prod,topology=multi-primary

code_snippets.title: Pre-migration checklist (MySQL  summary)
code_snippets.content:
1. Confirm source MySQL version and enabled plugins.
2. Capture a full logical backup of all schemas in scope.
3. Validate network connectivity and firewall rules between application, MySQL, and the target cluster.

configuration_parameters.name: max_clients
configuration_parameters.description: Maximum number of concurrent client connections the server will accept before refusing new connections.

configuration_parameters.name: statement_timeout
configuration_parameters.description: Maximum duration a single statement is allowed to run before it is automatically cancelled.

configuration_parameters.name: shared_buffers
configuration_parameters.description: Amount of memory the server uses for shared data buffers, affecting cache hit ratios.

configuration_parameters.name: work_mem
configuration_parameters.description: Memory available for internal sort operations and hash tables before writing temporary data to disk.

configuration_parameters.name: max_replication_slots
configuration_parameters.description: Maximum number of replication slots that can be created on the server for physical or logical replication.

error_code_environment_details.description: In production, DB-1042 typically indicates a misconfiguration in TLS protocols, certificates, or cipher suites between application clients and the database cluster.
During development, DB-1042 often occurs when using self-signed certificates or local test endpoints without properly configuring the client trust store.
In production environments, DB-1305 means that a scheduled or manual backup did not complete successfully, which may impact recovery point objectives (RPO).
In staging, DB-1305 usually signals issues with test backup destinations or credentials and should be resolved before promoting changes to production.
